message:
feat(snapshot_engine): Cx-first capture_with_cx + ensure_session_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds the final missing Cx-first entry points on SnapshotEngine —
`capture_with_cx` (the core snapshot method) and its internal helper
`ensure_session_with_cx`. Together with tick 2's
`shutdown_checkpoint_with_cx` and tick 8's `cleanup_with_cx`,
SnapshotEngine is now fully Cx-first on every public async surface.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs (+217 src, +94 tests)

   a. `SnapshotError::Cancelled` variant added.

      Distinct from `NoChanges` / `InProgress` / `NoPanes` /
      `PaneList` / `Database` / `Serialization` — surfaces that the
      caller's capability context was cancelled before or during
      the capture. Operators can distinguish "the outer scope
      abandoned the capture" from "something else went wrong".

   b. `capture_with_cx(cx, panes, trigger) -> Result<Snapshot, _>`

      Mirrors `capture` with three Cx-first changes:

        * Pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()` BEFORE the in-progress guard
          is taken, so a canceled caller returns `Cancelled` without
          blocking other captures.

        * Pre-blocking-DB `cx.checkpoint()` before `spawn_blocking_db`
          so a mid-capture cancellation skips the synchronous DB
          write entirely.

        * Internal `last_state_hash` RwLock acquires use
          `read_with_cx(cx)` / `write_with_cx(cx)` (primitives from
          tick 9) instead of ambient `read()/write()`.

      Observability: `captures_attempted` still increments on the
      pre-cancel path (parity with legacy) so operators can see how
      many captures the engine tried. `captures_succeeded` and
      `panes_captured` only increment on actual success.

      Legacy `capture` preserved; strictly additive.

   c. `ensure_session_with_cx(cx, topology_json, now_ms)` internal
      helper.

      Cx-bound session_id RwLock acquires + pre-blocking-DB
      checkpoint. Called by `capture_with_cx`. The legacy
      `ensure_session` is unchanged for `capture()` callers.

   d. `shutdown_checkpoint_with_cx` (from tick 2) now calls
      `capture_with_cx` instead of legacy `capture`. The
      match-arm also now handles `SnapshotError::Cancelled` as a
      best-effort-mark-shutdown / return-None branch.

2. Two new tests:

   * `capture_with_cx_pre_cancelled_returns_cancelled`: pre-cancels
     the Cx, asserts `Err(Cancelled)`, asserts `captures_attempted`
     incremented by 1 but `captures_succeeded` unchanged, AND
     verifies the in-progress guard is released (a subsequent
     `capture()` succeeds).

   * `capture_with_cx_happy_path_matches_legacy_capture`: fresh Cx,
     asserts `SnapshotResult` shape + `captures_succeeded` /
     `panes_captured` counters increment. No-regression pin.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib snapshot_engine::: 89/89 pass
    (87 after tick 8 + 2 new Cx-first)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

SnapshotEngine is now the SIXTH multi-method surface to reach full
Cx-first parity (after CautClient, ProtocolRecoveryEngine, CassClient,
RecordingManager, Pool). Every public async entry point on the engine
now has an explicit Cx variant.

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
